preview.summary: Top-ranked Spain face Saudi Arabia in a Group H meeting that could shape qualification. Spain's slick possession game meets a Saudi side rebuilding under a late coaching change, hoping to spring the kind of upset they managed against Argentina in 2022.
preview.homeForm: Spain top the FIFA rankings as European and Olympic champions, unbeaten across their last five with three wins and two draws.
preview.awayForm: Saudi Arabia, ranked around 32nd, qualified by topping their AFC group but arrive with limited recent form and a coaching change months before kickoff.
preview.homeKey: Pedri, the metronomic Barcelona midfielder dictating Spain's tempo.
preview.awayKey: Salem Al-Dawsari, Al-Hilal winger and the side's main creative spark.
storylines: Saudi Arabia replaced manager Hervé Renard just two months before the tournament
Spain chase a perfect group-stage start in Atlanta
Roberto Mancini's earlier project gives way to new leadership for the Green Falcons
preview.h2h: Spain have won all three previous meetings, scoring nine goals, including a World Cup victory.
